elements.name: Patient positioning (supine)
elements.textAnnotation: Supine position

elements.name: Pneumoperitoneum
elements.textAnnotation: Left subcostal access with Verres needle, To ~ 15 mmHg

elements.name: Marking of ports’ positions
elements.textAnnotation: 3x 8 mm and 1x 12 mm ports in a line three cross fingers under the costal arch (for R2, 8mm: on the midline slightly above or at the umbilicus; for R3, 8 mm: on the left mid-clavicular line (MCL) at least 8 cm away from R2 on a straight line; for R4, 8 mm: as far left lateral from R3 as possible; for R1, 12 mm: on right MCL at least 8 cm away from R2); 1x 12-mm assisting port triangulated midway inferior between R2 and R1; 1x 5 mm port for liver retractor subcostally on the right MCL

elements.name: Initial diagnostic laparoscopy
elements.textAnnotation: Rule out peritoneal carcinomatosis or liver metastases
Suspected peritoneal carcinomatosis or liver metastasis

elements.name: Liver retraction
elements.textAnnotation: Elevation of the right liver lobe

elements.name: Patient positioning (15° reverse Trendelenburg)
elements.textAnnotation: Table in reverse Trendelenburg position (~ 15°) and a ~ 10° rotation to the right

elements.name: Da Vinci docking and targeting the hiatus
elements.textAnnotation: R1: Fenestrated bipolar forceps, R2: 30° endoscope, R3: Permanent cautery hook or EndoWrist® Vessel Sealer, R4: Tip-up fenestrated grasper

elements.name: heading: Gastric and Distal Esophagus Mobilisation

elements.textAnnotation: Are disturbing adhesions present?
Too many abdominal adhesions

elements.name: Incision of lesser omentum up to right crus of diaphragm
Dissection of hiatus and gastrophrenic ligament
elements.textAnnotation: To the upper pole of the spleen including upper small gastric vessels
Persisting hiatal hernia

elements.name: Dissection of greater omentum with a medial to left lateral approach
elements.textAnnotation: Dissection until cardial notch is reached,Caution:Preservation of the gastroepiploic vascular arcade

elements.name: Dissection and ligation of retro-gastric adhesions and short gastric arteries

elements.textAnnotation: Hemostasis because of bleeding
Do you want to perform the step"Resection of splenic artery lymph nodes"?

elements.name: Resection of splenic artery lymph nodes
elements.textAnnotation: Resection of lymph node station 17
Hemostasis because of bleeding

elements.name: Dissection of greater omentum with a medial to right lateral approach and mobilization of hepatic flexure
elements.textAnnotation: Dissection until duodenum is visible, Mobilization of the hepatic flexure as far as needed to allow tension free movement of the pylorus, Caution: Preservation of the right gastroepiploic vascular arcade
Injuring the right gastroepiploic vascular arcade
Do you want to perform the  step"Dorsal gastrolysis until gastroduodenal artery is reached"?

elements.name: Dorsal gastrolysis until gastroduodenal artery is reached
Dissection and ligation of right gastric artery

elements.textAnnotation: Hemostasis because of bleeding
Does the patient have a gallbladder?
Do you want to perform the step"Cholecystectomy"?

elements.name: Cholecystectomy
elements.textAnnotation: Caution:Do not injure the right hepatic artery
Hemostasis because of  njuring the right hepatic artery

elements.name: heading:  Gastric Tube Construction and Lymphadenectomy
Gastric tube construction
elements.textAnnotation: Start at the Crow`s foot stapling (DaVinci left hand) the stomach vertical to the small gastric curvature, then stapling multiple times parallel to the great gastric curvature to create the initial part of the tube; 4,5 cm diameter
Hemostasis because of bleeding
Do you want to perform the step"Dorsal gastrolysis until gastroduodenal artery is reached"?

elements.name: Dorsal gastrolysis until gastroduodenal artery is reached
Resection of lymph nodes along hepatoduodenal ligament and common hepatic artery
elements.textAnnotation: Resection of lymph node stations 19 and 18, Resection of lymph nodes along hepatoduodenal ligament until portal vein
Resection of  Common hepatic artery lymph nodes
Hemostasis because of bleeding
Resection of  Hepatoduodenal ligament lymph nodes
Do you want to perform the step"Resection of splenic artery lymph nodes"?

elements.name: Resection of splenic artery lymph nodes
elements.textAnnotation: Resection of lymph node station 17
Hemostasis because of bleeding
Hemostasis because of bleeding

elements.name: Resection of left gastric artery lymph nodes and ligation of left gastric artery
elements.textAnnotation: Resection of lymph node station 15
Hemostasis because of bleeding

elements.name: Resection of celiac trunk lymph nodes nodes up to diaphragm
elements.textAnnotation: Resection of lymph node station 16
Hemostasis because of bleeding
Does the patient have a gallbladder?

elements.name: Cholecystectomy
elements.textAnnotation: Caution: Do not injure the right hepatic artery
Hemostasis because of  injuring the right hepatic artery

elements.name: Dissection of the gastroesophageal junction from the hiatus resecting lymphatic tissue until aorta is reached dorsally
elements.textAnnotation: Resection of paracardial lymph nodes (lymph node station 14 R&L)
Resection of  Paracardial lymph nodes

elements.name: Transhiatal ligation of thoracic duct on the right side of the aorta
Transhiatal incision of right pleura and pulmonary ligament resecting lymphatic tissue laterally along pleura
elements.textAnnotation: Resection of pulmonary ligament lymph nodes (lymph node station 13 R&L) If possible: do not open the left pleura to prevent hiatal hernias
Resection of  Pulmonary ligament lymph nodes
Hemostasis because of injuring the heart, the aorta

elements.name: Ventrally dissection along pericardium resecting lymphatic tissue
elements.textAnnotation: Partly resection of lower mediastinal paraesophageal lymph nodes (lymph node station 12 R&L)
Resection of  Lower mediastinal paraesohageal lymph nodes
Hemostasis because of injuring the heart
Does the patient have a lot of fluid at the operative site?

elements.name: Patient positioning (left lateral decubitus)
elements.textAnnotation:
Left lateral decubitus position with the table in a modified prone position
(~ 45°rotation of the lateral chest)

elements.name: Marking the ports' positions
elements.textAnnotation: For R4 in the 4th intercostal space (ICS), 1 cm medial to the scapula; for R3 in the 6th ICS, slightly more medial than port 4; for R2 in the 8th ICS at the same level as port 4; for R1 on the scapular line or slightly posterior in the 10th ICS. A 6–10- cm spacing is maintained between ports; for the assisting port in the 9th ICS triangulated between R1 and R2 (this would be the site for specimen removal and entrance of circular stapler) or triangulated between R3 and R4 in the 5th ICS

elements.name: CO2 Insufflation
elements.textAnnotation: To ~ 8 mmHg
Lung texture affected

elements.name: Placement of other ports
elements.textAnnotation: 4x 8 mm ports for robotic arms

elements.name: Da Vinci docking and targeting the azygos vein arcus
elements.textAnnotation: R1: Tip-up fenestrated grasper, R2: Fenestrated bipolar forceps, R3: 30° endoscope, R4: Permanent cautery hook or Monopolar curved scissors or EndoWrist® Vessel Sealer

elements.name: Incision of mediastinal pleura above and below azygos vein arcus
Ligation of azygos vein arcus to superior vena cava

elements.textAnnotation: Hemostasis because of injuring the azygos vein

elements.name: Incision of pleura dorsally along azygos vein with lymphadenectomy along the aorta to the hiatus
elements.textAnnotation: From 3-4 cm above the azygos vein arcus to the hiatus., Resection of middle and lower mediastinal paraesophageal lymph nodes (lymph node stations 11 R, 12 R)
Resection of  Middle mediastinal paraesophageal lymph nodes
Hemostasis because of injuring the aorta,the azygos vein
Resection of  Lower mediastinal paraesohageal lymph nodes

elements.name: Ligation of thoracic duct at the level of the hiatus right to the aorta
Incision of pleura ventrally starting at the transhiatal dissection point with lymphadenectomy along pericardium, left atrium and lung to right main bronchus and carina
elements.textAnnotation: Resection of lower and middle mediastinal paraesophageal and subcarinal lymph nodes (lymph node stations 12 L, 11 L, 9)
Resection of  Subcarinal lymph nodes
Hemostasis because of injuring the pericardium, the aorta
Resection of  Middle mediastinal paraesophageal lymph nodes
Resection of  Lower mediastinal paraesohageal lymph nodes

elements.name: Completion of lymphadenectomy along left main bronchus and around esophagus
elements.textAnnotation: Resection of aortopulmonary window, upper mediastinal paraesophageal, lower and upper paratracheal lymph nodes (lymph node stations 8, 10 R&L, 7 R&L, 6 R&L)
Resection of  Lower paratracheal lymph nodes
Resection of Upper paratracheal lymph nodes
Resection of Aortopulmonary window lymph nodes
Resection of  Upper mediastinal paratracheal lymph nodes
Hemostasis because of bleeding

elements.name: Transection of esophagus
elements.textAnnotation: At the height of the ligated azygos vein arcus

elements.name: Resection of the oral staple line with opening of the esophagus for frozen section

elements.textAnnotation: Section is not cancer free

elements.name: Construction of four holding sutures and a purse-string suture
elements.textAnnotation: Four holding sutures at 12, 3, 6 and 9 o`clock with fixation of the esophageal mucosa to the muscular wall. Purse-string suture for fixation of the anvil.

elements.name: heading: Stomach Pull-up and Insertion of Anvil
Mini-thoracotomy and insertion of an alexis wound retractor
Insertion of circular stapler anvil and securing it with purse-string suture

elements.textAnnotation: Circular stapler anvil does not fit into the esophageal stump
Threads of the sutures break

elements.name: Stomach pull-up under visual control
elements.textAnnotation: Pull-up until the gastric tube is located in the right thoracic cavity
False estimation of the tractive force necessary for stomach pull-up --> damaging the gastric conduit

elements.name: Extraction of specimen and incision in minor curvature
elements.textAnnotation: Out of the thorax through the mini-thoracotomy

elements.name: Insertion of circular stapler handle
elements.textAnnotation: 28mm tri-stapler into the stomach conduit bringing it back to the thorax

elements.name: Stapling the gastric conduit to the esophageal stump
Checking the perfusion of the gastric conduit with ICG

elements.textAnnotation: Anastomosis insufficiency (visible with ICG)

elements.name: Oversewing and checking again with ICG
Da Vinci re-docking and targeting the esophago-gastrostomy
Removal of specimen through the mini thoracotomy
Transection of gastric conduit left to the esophago-gastrostomy using a linear stapler
elements.textAnnotation: Separate dissection of the greater omentum and gastroepiploic vessels.
D2 -Lymphadenectomy
D3 -Lymphadenectomy

elements.name: Insertion of a gastric tube under visual control
Insertion of two chest tubes
elements.textAnnotation: One along the esophago-gastrostomy, one ventrally on the diaphragmatic dome for air

elements.name: Re-ventilation of the right lung under visual control
Closure of thorax, fascia and skin
